css: #dynamic-content .prompt-card { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #fdfbfb 0%, #f6eff0 100%); width: 100%; max-width: 100%; border-radius: 0px; box-shadow: 0 15px 45px rgba(0,0,0,0.05), 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.02); padding: 2.5rem 10px; margin: 0 auto 3rem; } #dynamic-content .pin-title { font-size: inherit; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: normal; margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 2rem; width: 100%; max-width: 100%; text-align: center; } #dynamic-content .description-container { margin-bottom: 2.5rem; text-align: center; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.05); padding-bottom: 1.5rem; } #dynamic-content .description-text { font-size: 1.05rem; color: #444; line-height: 1.6; font-style: italic; max-width: 44rem; margin: 0 auto; text-align: left; padding: 0 1.25rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-list { width: 100%; margin: 0 auto; } #dynamic-content .prompt-item { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 2rem; gap: 0.5rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-number { font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 800; color: #b8b8b8; padding-top: 2px; min-width: 1.5rem; text-align: left; } #dynamic-content .prompt-text { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 600; color: #222; line-height: 1.6; flex: 1; } @media (min-width: 768px) { #dynamic-content .prompt-card { max-width: 52rem; border-radius: 1.5rem; padding: 4.5rem 3.5rem; margin: 3rem auto 5rem; } #dynamic-content .pin-title { margin-bottom: 2.5rem; } #dynamic-content .description-container { margin-bottom: 3.5rem; padding-bottom: 2rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-item { gap: 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 2.75rem; } #dynamic-content .prompt-number { font-size: 1.25rem; min-width: 2.2rem; text-align: right; } #dynamic-content .prompt-text { font-size: 1.15rem; } }
html:
10 Calming Journal Prompts for Managing Daily Anxiety

Managing daily anxiety is easier when you have a simple space to process your thoughts. These 10 prompts help you relax, focus on the present, and feel more in control of your day.

1.
What is one worry you have right now? If you replaced that worry with a more helpful thought, what would that new thought be?
2.
Write down the one anxious thought bothering you most right now. Next to it, write why this thought is no longer serving you. Then, write one important task you can focus on instead.
3.
Think of one simple activity that helps you relax. How does your body feel when you do it, and why does that feeling help you right now?
4.
Think of a hard day you handled well in the past. What is one strength you used then that can help you get through today?
5.
What is one task that feels too big right now? If you only focus on the very first, tiny step, does it feel easier to start?
6.
What is one small, good thing that happened in the last 24 hours? Why did it make you feel better?
7.
Which part of your room or space feels the most calm? Describe what you see and hear there that helps you feel at ease.
8.
If a friend felt exactly like you do right now, what kind advice would you give them? Can you say those same words to yourself?
9.
Pick one quiet sound you can hear right now that you usually ignore. Describe the rhythm or tone of that sound and how it helps you stay calm.
10.
What is one worry you want to leave on this page so your mind can rest? How does it feel to see that thought outside of your head?
